page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.g6a family. It is built on the KVM hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical AMD EPYC 7H12 cores running at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The system includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Hardware capabilities include support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions. However, it lacks AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets. The instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is capped at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In terms of performance, benchmark data indicates limitations under heavy compute loads. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 756.09 falls into the bottom 10% of tested instances, while the multi-core score of 3045.4 is categorized as weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This suggests a qualitative tradeoff where the instance offers dedicated physical cores and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, but lower raw compute throughput per resource compared to newer hardware generations.

Given these specifications and performance metrics, the ecs.g6a.2xlarge is positioned for general-purpose workloads that require a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio but do not demand high-performance computing. Suitable workloads include standard web serving, application servers, microservices, and development environments that can operate within the 2 Gbps network baseline and do not require local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show limitations, with stress-ng single-core results in the bottom 10% and multi-core results in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile offers a balanced resource allocation for standard workloads but exhibits lower compute density. It is best utilized for general-purpose applications, development environments, and web serving where high-performance computing and local storage are not required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, showing lower relative CPU benchmark performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance offering a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for standard workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
No local storage and no integrated GPU accelerators
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
Lower relative CPU performance based on stress-ng benchmarks
Suited for general-purpose web serving and development environments
